transcript: push warm surface waters towards Asia, piling it up in the Western Pacific. In some years, though, the trade winds weaken, the warm surface water moves eastward and reduces upwelling of cold water off the coast of South America. Climatologists call this El Niño. Its climate impacts show up mostly in the wintertime over North America. The warmer ocean fuels an intensification and southward shift of the jet stream. This brings flooding to the southern United States and warmer, drier seas.
